diagrams.title: Design Thinking Process
diagrams.description: A circular five-stage iterative process showing Empathize → Define → Ideate → Prototype → Test, with arrows connecting back to Empathize indicating continuous iteration
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize how design thinking is not linear but iterative, with each stage informing the others

diagrams.title: Three Levels of Emotional Design
diagrams.description: Pyramid diagram with Visceral at base (immediate impression), Behavioral in middle (during use), and Reflective at top (after use), showing how they build upon each other
diagrams.purpose: Shows how all three levels must be considered for complete emotional design impact

diagrams.title: Fitts's Law Illustration
diagrams.description: Shows a user reaching for targets of different sizes and distances, with time indicators showing larger, closer targets take less time
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates why button size and placement matter for usability

diagrams.title: Gestalt Principles Summary
diagrams.description: Visual grid showing all five Gestalt principles with examples: Proximity (grouped items), Similarity (same-colored items), Closure (incomplete circle), Continuity (aligned dots), Figure-Ground (two faces or vase)
diagrams.purpose: Quick visual reference for applying Gestalt principles in layouts

diagrams.title: User-Centered Design Cycle
diagrams.description: Shows iterative UCD process: Understand User → Specify Requirements → Design Solutions → Evaluate → (back to Understand)
diagrams.purpose: Shows that UCD is an ongoing iterative process, not a one-time activity
